data.title: RTX Corporation: I Maintain A Buy Rating Despite Apparent Imbalance In Defense
data.description:
The financial year for the Department of Defense ended on the 30th of September. That provides a good moment to see how much contract value has been secured by primary defense contractors. The reason why I believe it's important to assess contracting activity for individual companies is because generally the view is that expanding defense budgets provide investors and analysts with a generic bull case for defense contractors. However, not all companies are seeing significant growth. In fact, out of all the defense contractors for which I have analyzed the contracting activity in the past DoD fiscal year, we saw that troubled aerospace and defense giant The Boeing Company (BA) was the only company that saw a significant increase in contracting activity while General Dynamics Corporation (GD) and Lockheed Martin Corporation (LMT) saw contracting activity decline.

In this report, I will be focusing on the contract flow for RTX Corporation (NYSE:RTX). The report will not be focused on a price target or rating for the stock. If you're interested in a price target assessment, I refer to a previous report discussing the earnings and valuation of RTX Corp.

RTX Corporation Contract Value Declines But Not At An Alarming Rate

Using the evoX Defense Contracts Monitor, The Aerospace Forum collects defense contract data for more than 100 companies and RTX Corporation is one of the companies that we cover. The database for which I have developed a visualizing software contains more than 11,000 contracts, which we leverage to cover aerospace and defense companies on a more detailed level. RTX Corporation secured $20.2 billion worth of contracts during the DoD fiscal year, which was a reduction of 26.6%. However, the number of contracts declined only 11.6%, showing that the decline was driven by a combination of lower volumes and lower value per contract. Obligated funds declined by 32.7% to $9.8 billion.

In total, there has been a $7.3 billion reduction in contract value. Interestingly, using our data analytics tools we can actually pinpoint the specific programs that caused the reduction, and it's not as bad as it seems. The F-35 program saw a $2.6 billion decline in contracting activity, and given the challenges Lockheed Martin faced on the F-35 program this year getting the TR-3 or Technology Refresh 3 ready, a lower contract value does not really come as a surprise. Furthermore, there is always some lumpiness in the contracting activity for the program, and it takes time for demand to translate to firm contracts. So, in many cases, the increased interest in the F-35 is not yet reflected in the order activity.

Furthermore, last year there was a $5.5 billion framework contract that RTX won for services on the F117 engine which is used on the C-17 Globemaster III, and the Patriot system saw a decline in contracting activity of $1 billion. This was partially offset by higher radar sales and missile defense contracts of which the LTAMDS experienced the most notable contract growth.

There's also a different way to look at the changes and that's by excluding the IDIQs which are indefinite-delivery/indefinite-quantity contracts. These contract vehicles provide a framework against which task and delivery orders can be placed. If we exclude these contract vehicles, the decline in the value of the contracts is around 21%.

RTX Corporation Has A Diversified Product Portfolio

One thing that I definitely do like about RTX Corporation is the fact that its order inflow is nicely diversified. Missiles and missile defense account for the majority of the company's contract flow and that's a part of the defense business where we increased demand due to the development of new weapon system capabilities such as hypersonics. And missile defense and radars are also higher in demand as the increased threat levels are triggering US and US allies to increase defense capabilities. So, the missile, missile defense and radar and communications & intelligence parts of the defense space show the defensive stance as well as the demand for the development of deterrents. Furthermore, via engine platforms, the company has significant exposure to aircraft including fighter jets such as the F-35 for which demand is increasing as more and more countries are increasing budgets. Previously, the F-35 was deemed too expensive for many countries. But with increased defense budgets, the F-35 now becomes an option for many countries and that provides RTX with contracts to provide the engines and a long-term sustained tail.

By the end of the second quarter, RTX had a backlog of $206 billion, of which $77 billion is attributable to the defense business. For RTX, it's rather difficult to assess whether the current backlog is a comfortable one and the reason for that is that its reporting segments are not pure commercial sales or pure defense sales. However, when we look at the annualized sales for the Raytheon segment, which is the best reflection of performance in the defense business, we see that annualized sales should be around $26.35 billion. I consider a backlog of 2x annual sales to be comfortable, and RTX Corporation actually gets to 2.9x and that's when we don't account for military sales for Collins Aerospace and Pratt & Whitney. Adding the military sales of the other segments, we get to $14.2 billion in annualized sales. Adding this to the $26.35 billion for Raytheon gives us $40.6 billion in annualized sales and brings the backlog-to-sales to 1.9x. So, the ratio is slightly below the 2x targeted, but I don't think it is alarming as the company also has IDIQs which could materialize into firm contracts. However, I do note that measured against the contract flow observed in the past DoD fiscal year the book-to-bill ratio only reaches 0.5x. So, RTX actually needs a significant step-up in contract activity to maintain a comfortable cushion.

Conclusion: Increased Demand For Defense Equipment Not Clearly Visible For RTX

What holds for RTX is that it has a diversified defense portfolio, but its $77 billion backlog falls short of the comfortable cushion of 2x and its order inflow for defense contracts has been significantly lower. The Raytheon segment, which is fully dedicated to defense, has a 12-month rolling book-to-bill ratio of 1.13x, signaling healthy demand. But I believe that the company requires a significantly higher order inflow to maintain a comfortable cushion and execute growth in the defense business. So, with that in mind, I'm turning a bit more cautious on RTX regarding growth in the defense business, but I maintain bullish on the overall combination of defense and commercial sales.

data.title: Wave Life Sciences: Positive WVE-006 Data For AATD Leads To 2025 Catalyst
data.description:
Wave Life Sciences (NASDAQ:WVE) announced positive results from its phase 1b/2a RestorAAtion-2 study using WVE-006 for the treatment of patients with alpha-1 antitrypsin deficiency [AATD]. It was shown that a single subcutaneous administration of 200 mg of this therapy was able to result in the presence of wild-type alpha-1 antitrypsin [M-AAT] protein. This ended up resulting in the creation of more than 60% of total AAT protein. This is very good news, but because this catalyst has passed doesn't mean that there isn't another catalyst to look forward to for this program. That is, the company expects to release additional data from this very same mid-stage study in 2025. Besides this, there is another candidate who recently had news released as well. This would be with respect to a clinical candidate by the name of WVE-003 for the treatment of patients with Huntington's Disease.

The company has been able to report a statistically significant reduction of cerebral spinal fluid [CSF] mHTT protein for them. However, despite this achievement, Takeda Pharmaceutical (TAK) chose not to exercise its option for this program anyways. On the flip side, Wave Life Sciences now has full rights to this program. Not only that, but it is still expected to meet with the FDA before the end of 2024 to discuss the possibility of an Accelerated Approval pathway for it as well. With additional results from the phase 1b/2a RestorAAtion-2 study using WVE-006 for AATD patients, plus the possibility of having an Accelerated Approval pathway of WVE-003 for the treatment of patients with HD, I believe that investors could benefit from any potential gains made here.

WVE-006 Has Another Expected Milestone To Unlock Further Value

As I noted above, Wave Life Sciences is developing the use of WVE-006 for the treatment of patients with alpha-1 Antitrypsin Deficiency in the ongoing phase 1b/2a RestorAAtion-2 study. Before going over this mid-stage trial, plus any catalysts to come from this program, it is first important to understand what this disorder is and what the possible market opportunity for it could be. Alpha-1 Antitrypsin Deficiency [AATD] is an inherited disorder that may cause lung or liver disease. What happens is that these patients don't have enough AAT protein to protect both of these organs. Why is that? It is because such patients inherit two copies of the SERPINA1 gene. This gene causes the formation of abnormal AAT proteins and thus, functioning AAT protein levels are not ideal. The global Alpha-1 Antitrypsin Deficiency market is expected to reach a $10 billion market value by 2033. This is a large market opportunity and the company is well on its way to potentially capture it. Especially, with the positive data it has released from this study.

In order to see if WVE-006 is capable of being able to treat patients with AATD, it is in the process of running the phase 1b/2a RestorAAtion-2 study. This trial is expected to recruit up to a total of 24 patients who are to be dosed with WVE-006. The thing to note is that the data just released today only deals with a single subcutaneous dose of this GalNAc-conjugated drug. Having said that, even though this was just a single dose, two patients were able to achieve more than 60% of total AAT protein levels. Again, this is very good news, because it goes back to the notion that these patients lack AAT. Not only that, but there was the presence of M-ATT protein confirmed. Why is this crucial? It is because this shows that there was successful editing of mutant Z-AAT mRNA. In turn, the ability to generate 50% M-AAT in this heterozygous "MZ" genotype patients, means lower risk of AATD liver and lung disease. The thing is that there might be an opportunity for the data to eventually be improved upon. That's because the expectation is that the company expects to release multi-dose WVE-006 data, targeting these patients with AATD, in 2025. If the single dose data was able to achieve this, then it is quite possible that the multi-dose data may end up doing better.

Financials

According to the 10-Q SEC Filing, Wave Life Sciences had cash and cash equivalents of $154 million as of June 30th of 2024. The projection was that it would have enough cash to fund its operations into Q4 of 2025. This is about a little over 1-year's worth of cash runway, having said that the company decided to raise additional funds for its operations. Having said that, it was able to complete an upsized public offering to raise approximately $230 million before deducting expenses.

An important item to mention is that this doesn't include any potential milestone and other payments under the collaboration agreement it has with GlaxoSmithKline (GSK). Both of these companies are advancing WVE-006 for the treatment of patients with AATD together in a partnership. Thus, once this phase 1b/2a RestorAAtion-2 study concludes, then from there, GlaxoSmithKline will be responsible for development and commercialization of this drug for the treatment of this patient population. If this program goes well, then Wave Life Sciences is eligible to receive up to $525 million in milestone payments along with tiered royalties on net sales.

Risks To Business

There are several risks that investors should be aware of before investing in Wave Life Sciences. The first risk to consider deals with the ongoing phase 1b/2a RestorAATion-2 study, which is using WVE-006 for the treatment of patients with AATD. Even though the initial subcutaneous single-dose data turned out to be positive, there is no guarantee that the multi-dose results to be released in 2025 will turn out to be positive as well. Nor, that the efficacy of the drug will be improved upon with further dosing.

The second risk to consider would be in terms of the development of WVE-003 for the treatment of patients with HD. That's because the goal is to bring the data established thus far to regulatory authorities to see if it can obtain an Accelerated Approval pathway. The risk here is that there is no assurance that one or any regulatory agency will allow for an Accelerated Approval pathway based on the clinical evidence obtained thus far.

The third and final risk to consider would be in terms of the advancement of WVE-007 for the treatment of patients with obesity. The goal is to develop this drug either alone as a monotherapy or in combination with a GLP-1 agonist to treat them. This oligonucleotide is a small interfering RNA [GalNAc-siRNA] to silence the Inhibin βE gene. If this is silenced, then it would induce fat-burning along with muscle mass preservation. The risk is that there is no assurance that the drug will work either alone or in combination with a GLP-1 agonist to treat the obesity patients. Plus, a phase 1 study using WVE-007 to treat these patients is expected to begin in 2025. Upon completion of this trial, there is no guarantee that the results will turn out to be positive.

Conclusion

Wave Life Science has done well to prove that its RNA editing platform is capable of treating a variety of disorders. It has shown to do well in treating these patients with Huntington's Disease [HD] by showing the ability of WVE-003 to reduce CSF mHTT protein by 46%. Even though Takeda didn't exercise its option to take over this program, there is still a milestone in play for investors to consider. In that, the company is expected to meet with multiple regulatory agencies to see if it can obtain an Accelerated Approval pathway for this program before the end of 2024. It has also shown its technology to help patients with AATD as well. Not only were positive results just released today using WVE-006 to treat these patients, but there is another data set expected from it in 2025. I believe that this biotech has established proof of mechanism of action [MOA] in several disorders. Thus, I believe it has set itself up for long-term success.

Editor's Note: This article discusses one or more securities that do not trade on a major U.S. exchange. Please be aware of the risks associated with these stocks.

data.title: Nature's Sunshine Products: Cheap, But Not Entirely Without A Reason To Be
data.description:
Self-care is becoming increasingly important to everyday Americans. In fact, globally, it is more important now than ever. In the US alone, in 2022, about 50% of consumers reported wellness as a top priority in their day-to-day lives. That's up from 42% just two years earlier. This is especially the case when it comes to younger generations. Millennials, for instance, are very wellness oriented. 52% of them stated that they have a high prioritization that they are assigning to health and wellness. That compares to 47% for all consumers. Better nutrition comes in at 48% compared to 40% for consumers. And better appearance is at 37% compared to 30% for consumers. In fact, in 2022, the total US wellness products market was worth an estimated $450 billion. At each year, it's expected to grow by about 5%.

In the Asia Pacific part of the world, the overall industry is even larger. While the total North American wellness market is worth about $1.3 trillion, in the Asia Pacific region, it's a massive $1.5 trillion. In fact, in Asia, wellness is such a big thing that it has even been the epicenter of what is now known as the wellness tourism industry. One company that is focused very much on Wellness, not only throughout North America, but also in Asia and elsewhere, is Nature's Sunshine Products (NASDAQ:NATR). With a market capitalization of only $243.8 million, the business is quite small. However, it has a robust balance sheet and shares are trading at remarkably low multiples. The one downside to this is that financial results are volatile and have exhibited no signs of real growth over the past few years. But even with these weak spots, the stock looks cheap enough to maintain as a soft ‘buy’.

A wellness check on this name

Whenever I write an article about a company for the first time, I like to dig in and really discuss what the company is and what it does. For those not familiar with Nature's Sunshine Products, management describes the firm as a natural health and wellness business. It's focused largely on the production of nutritional and personal care products. These are sold directly to customers. But they are also sold to a sales force of independent consultants, who then resell those products to customers.

While the wellness space is truly vast in scope, I think it would be appropriate to use that same description for Nature's Sunshine Products and what it offers. Based on what management has said, the company sells over 800 products that touch on the immune system, cardiovascular health, digestive health, general personal care, weight management, and more. Using herbs and other things, the company creates and then packages these products that it then sells to its customers. For all intents and purposes, these products can be classified as dietary supplements.

The supplement industry represents only a very small piece of the overall global wellness market. However, it is a space that has been growing at a nice rate. From 2019 through 2023, sales expanded from $142.5 million to $182.7 million. That's an annualized growth rate of 6.4%. This year, the market should grow further to $192.4 million. And by 2026, it's expected to hit $214 million. Usually small companies like this that are based in the US get most of their sales from the US. Or if not, at least they get most of their sales from North America. However, Nature's Sunshine Products is an exception to this. In 2023, 45.2% of the company's sales came from Asia. North America had the second-largest contribution at 31.4%. Europe came in third at 18.2%. And the rest of the world, comprising mostly of the Latin America region, accounted for the remaining 5.2% of sales.

In the chart above, you can see growth that the company has achieved from a revenue perspective by region. Asia has been the major source of expansion for the company during this time. In 2021, the company generated $176.9 million in revenue from there. By 2023, that number had grown to $201.3 million. Unfortunately, other parts of the world have not been as kind for Nature's Sunshine Products as Asia has. But starting in 2023, the firm did begin to see improvements in both North America and Europe. Unfortunately, for the current fiscal year, Asia has started to be a bit of a problem. This was mostly because of weakness in China caused by what management described as reduced activity coming as a result of challenging macroeconomic factors.

What matters most to me is that the business generates revenue from all of the most important parts of the world for this industry. In 2023, 36% of all global supplement sales came from North America. But Asia and Oceania were close behind at 34%. Europe and the Middle East are still large markets, accounting for a combined 19% of the space. And Latin America is responsible for about 10% of sales.

When it comes to Nature's Sunshine Products on its own, overall financial performance has been mixed in recent years. As I mentioned earlier, weakness in key markets, particularly in North America and Europe, caused revenue to drop from $444.1 million in 2021 to $421.9 million in 2022. A recovery in both North America and Europe, combined with continued strong growth in Asia that management attributed to a continued dedication to growing its sales force in markets like Taiwan and Japan, as well as to the firm furthering its digitally focused, livestream business model in China, was responsible for an expansion in sales to $445.3 million last year.

On the bottom line, as the chart above illustrates, financial performance has been all over the map. Net profits and cash flows have shown no real trend year over year. In fact, in both 2022 and 2023, adjusted operating cash flow remained flat at $28 million compared to the $53.5 million reported for 2021, while EBITDA for the company rebounded somewhat from 2022. To be honest with you, the profit and cash flow picture for the business does concern me more than anything else. Some of this, like a good chunk of the weakness experienced in 2022, can be attributed to really high-income taxes. That year, the effective tax rate for the business was an astounding 96.4%. But other factors have also proven problematic as well, namely an increase in selling, general, and administrative costs caused by higher compensation, marketing, and certain growth oriented variable costs.

If we were seeing rapid growth, I would actually be fine with some of this short-term pain. However, that is not the case. For the first half of this year, revenue was $221.5 million. That's down 1.6% compared to the $225.2 million reported one year earlier. This was mostly because of the aforementioned weakness in its Asia operations that management chalked up to China. Admittedly, the company did see its net profits improve from $3.3 million to $3.7 million. But other profitability metrics have been mixed. Operating cash flow dropped from $17.3 million to only $3.5 million. If we adjust for changes in working capital, we do get a nice increase from $11.6 million to $18 million. However, EBITDA for the company dropped year over year from $20.4 million to $19.6 million.

Another issue that I have with the company is that management seems to have no idea what financial results will look like even a short time out from the present moment. Previous guidance provided by the company called for revenue of between $455 million and $480 million for the 2024 fiscal year. But that range has since been reduced to between $436 million and $445 million. Meanwhile, EBITDA guidance has been reduced from between $42 million and $48 million to between $39 million and $42 million. If we take the midpoint there and assume that adjusted operating cash flow will rise at the same rate, we would get a reading for this year of $28.1 million.

Normally, this kind of volatility would cause me to rate a company a ‘hold’ or lower. But there are two positives that caused me to rate this business a soft ‘buy’. The first is the fact that the enterprise has no debt on its books and enjoys a net cash position of $68.7 million. Stacked up against a market capitalization of only $243.8 million, this is a massive amount of cash to have. And the second is that shares are quite cheap. Using the estimates for 2024 and the historical results for 2023, we can see in the chart above just how cheap shares are. I then compared the company to five similar firms, shown in the table below. On a price to operating cash flow basis, two of the five companies ended up being cheaper than Nature's Sunshine Products is. This drops to one of the five when using the EV to EBITDA scenario.

Company Price / Operating Cash Flow EV / EBITDA
Nature's Sunshine Products 8.7 4.5
The Beauty Health Company (SKIN) 99.8 40.4
Medifast (MED) 3.2 0.7
Coty Inc (COTY) 13.3 13.6
FitLife Brands (FTLF) 17.5 13.6
Edgewell Personal Care Company (EPC) 8.6 9.0

Takeaway

Based on the data provided, I definitely think that Nature's Sunshine Products is an interesting firm. I am encouraged by how cheap shares are, and I like its balance sheet. Management also seems to think that shares are undervalued, as evidenced by the fact that the company has recently repurchased $7.7 million worth of stock and has another $9.9 million of capacity under the share buyback program that's in place. These positives are offset to some extent by the historical volatility of the company, especially at a time when the industry that it operates is growing consistently. Because of that, the best I can rate the company is a soft ‘buy’.

data.title: Undercovered Dozen ETF Edition: Treasury Bonds, Uranium And Copper Miners, Undervalued AI +
data.description:
Introduction

The 'Undercovered' Dozen series is an effort to highlight undercovered stocks on our platform for you to have another source for idea generation. Today, we're focused explicitly on lesser covered exchange-traded funds.

The structure of the article will be the same as previous editions: seven articles are highlighted as primary features with an in-depth glimpse of the author's thesis provided as an excerpt, while five others are included with fewer details.

In this one, we are looking at ETFs from articles published between September 17th - October 15th.

Take a look at what these ideas might hold for you. And please join the conversation below to share what you think: are any of these worth following up on? And are there other undercovered ETFs that you like?

My Replacement For Cash: SHV

Colorado Wealth Management Fun | Hold

It's time for a quick look at the iShares Short Treasury Bond ETF (NASDAQ:SHV).

This is a very simple ETF. It holds short-term Treasuries. The duration is very low, and the credit quality is all Treasuries, so you can expect volatility to be extremely low.

The 52-week range is from $109.85 to $110.63. That's quite a bit less than a 1% move for the very wildest trades. You can see that it simply moves up before going ex-dividend, then drops on the ex-dividend date.

There are a few ETFs you can use for this kind of exposure. I find them particularly useful for parking cash, so I can earn a respectable interest rate.

Sure, there's a small expense ratio of .15%, but it's hard to do better. It beats leaving money in checking or savings to get paid less than a quarter of a percent. Maybe your bank pays out well, but that hasn't been my experience.

I keep most of my idle cash in my Charles Schwab account and put it in SHV. There are mutual fund options as well, but I've always been more familiar with using ETFs. Consequently, I use SHV to hold the majority of my cash.

QQQI ETF: Options Strategy Provides Better Capital Appreciation

Kevin Shan | Buy

Nearly two weeks ago, I wrote an article discussing the Global X Nasdaq 100® Covered Call & Growth ETF (QYLG). It's a fund I regarded as too simple with its options strategy, which isn't always good when selling covered calls. Far better NASDAQ 100 buy-write funds exist today, and it's only natural that I discuss some of them. One better alternative is the NEOS NASDAQ-100® High Income ETF (QQQI). This fund employs a more dynamic options strategy than its peers, leading to QQQI outperforming other NASDAQ 100 covered call funds while yielding 15.16% based on its most recent distributions. Of course, tradeoffs always exist, especially with yields in the double-digit range. However, the overall performance of QQQI overcomes some of these problems, justifying a Buy rating.

What separates QQQI from other NASDAQ 100 buy-write funds is its dynamic call option strategy. While NEOS will generally sell their call options out-of-the-money, the percentage out-of-the-money and the portion of the portfolio that's covered varies depending on market conditions. Little adjustments (as long as they're the right adjustments) can increase total returns without sacrificing too much on the distribution side. This is what QQQI has shown.

URNJ: The Demand For Uranium Continues To Grow

Bang For The Buck | Buy

The Sprott Junior Uranium Miners ETF (NASDAQ:URNJ) was launched in early 2023 as more of a pure play investment option for uranium miners. Sprott also has the Uranium Miners ETF (URNM), which is the larger of the two ETFs, and it has a history going back to late 2019. I have covered URNJ a few times since it was launched, and my prior articles on the ETF can be found here.

There is a lot of overlap between the two ETFs, but the main difference is that the two largest uranium mining companies Cameco (CCJ) & Kazatomprom and the two larger uranium investment vehicles the Sprott Physical Uranium Trust (OTCPK:SRUUF) & Yellow Cake (OTCQX:YLLXF) are not in URNJ. So, it invests exclusively in uranium mining companies, with a weighting towards the stocks below the largest ones in the industry.

Some reaction for uranium miners following the recent spot market weakness is understandable, but I would argue the long-term contract price is at least as important for uranium miners, and that has continued to show strength. So, I consider the current underperformance for URNJ excessive and has provided a great buying opportunity, especially given the positive developments in the nuclear industry.

COPX: A Good ETF In A Likely Overbought Copper Market

The Methodical Investor | Hold

Copper prices have been on a wild ride this past year, after oscillating for months around $3.75/lb, the price rocketed up past the $5 handle in May before eventually falling back below $4 in August. In recent days, however, it rallied again on the back of Bank of China stimulus news.

Given these heightened levels of price volatility, the first question then becomes: will this most recent rally have legs or will it eventually sputter out like the April-May price move eventually did? And the second question is whether the Global X Copper Miners ETF (COPX) is a good vehicle for investors to gain exposure to the copper sector.

The current case for copper is much stronger than that for many other industrial metals. The transition to electric vehicles, which I discussed at length in a previous article on Tesla Inc. (TSLA), as well as the concerted global move towards less carbon-intensive sources of electricity will require a lot more of it. In fact, the exhibit below shows how electric vehicles require more than double the amount of copper per vehicle than do vehicles with Internal Combustion Engines.

CHAT ETF: A Surprisingly Undervalued AI Fund

Hedge Insider | Strong Buy

Roundhill Generative AI & Technology ETF (CHAT) is an exchange-traded fund set up on May 18, 2023, to invest in companies that are contributing significantly either directly or indirectly to generative artificial intelligence innovations. The top 5 holdings include Nvidia Corp. (NVDA), Alphabet Inc. (GOOGL), Microsoft Corp. (MSFT), Meta Platforms Inc. (META), and Advanced Micro Devices Inc. (AMD). These holdings each represented around 4-7% of the overall portfolio, while the portfolio as a whole has 46 stocks.

The stock ticker "CHAT" is a clear reference to chat-based generative AI made famous since late 2022 by OpenAI's flagship product "ChatGPT", which is essentially an online portal through which users can access OpenAI's large language models ("LLMs"). However, generative AI extends beyond this self-limiting financial moniker, into video, audio, and multi-modal AI systems.

To be honest, I think the portfolio construction is a little primitive, since while it is broad and extends across the world, it forgets obvious would-be inclusions such as Tesla, Inc. (TSLA), which is working on some cutting-edge self-driving AI technology. Nevertheless, the portfolio is nicely balanced across various stocks, not heavily concentrated in just a couple of stocks, and the thematic is generally well represented.

SH ETF: An Effective Market Hedge With Hidden Income

Stuart Alssopp | Strong Buy

I believe that the ProShares Short S&P500 ETF (SH) offers a great way for investors to hedge against recent market gains and benefit from the income differential between short-term interest rates and the S&P500 dividend yield. While the SH has lost 19% over the past year, it has done so despite 34% total return gains in the S&P500, which should have theoretically resulted in a 25% SH decline. The difference reflects income received on its T-bill holdings as well as returns correlated to low volatility. These factors suggest that unless we see continued strong expansion of S&P500 valuation multiples, the SH should generate positive returns over the next 12 months, while any valuation compression could see the ETF surge higher.

While the SH ETF is seen as a way to benefit from short-term decline in the S&P500, it has performed well in the past over several years following periods when interest rates were significantly above the equity dividend yield as they are today. Over the past year, the SH has generated over 8% returns in excess of what would have been expected based on the performance of the S&P500, and we should expect a repeat of this over the next 12 months, strongly suggesting positive returns for the SH.

QDEF: A Low Volatility Dividend ETF Outperforming Peers

Carlos R. Tartarini | Buy

The FlexShares Quality Dividend Defensive Index Fund (QDEF) has a distinct approach among dividend-oriented funds. Unlike many funds that focus heavily on yields, QDEF seeks to structure a portfolio with a quality orientation and below-average volatility.

With an allocation relatively close to broader market indexes, QDEF maintains a large exposure to technology stocks but is underweight in energy and utilities, as opposed to typical dividend ETFs, which often carry heavy positions in these areas. This allocation profile has strongly contributed to QDEF's outperformance over its peers. As the outlook remains generally benign for the stock market, I have a positive view of this fund, given its potential to deliver capital gains in the long run with relatively limited volatility.

QDEF is a fund that provides access to high-quality, income-oriented U.S. mid and large-cap companies, targeting to achieve lower volatility than the broader market. It tracks the investment results of the Northern Trust Quality Dividend Defensive Index.

The Other Five Fit For Mention

SJNK: Low-Volatility Junk Bond ETF

Fred Piard | Sell

SPDR® Bloomberg Short Term High Yield Bond ETF (SJNK) aims to reduce risk by focusing on junk bonds with maturities under 5 years. SJNK is well-diversified across sectors and issuers, with a significant allocation to consumer cyclicals and industrials. Despite marginally outperforming a junk bond benchmark, SJNK has suffered significant value decay and underperformed "fallen angels" ETFs.

CWEB: Set For Re-Rating Once Deflation Stabilizes

Theme Catcher Invest | Buy

Direxion Daily CSI China Internet Bull 2x Shares ETF (CWEB) is a leveraged ETF focused on Chinese internet stocks, which act as proxies for China's mass consumption sector, including e-commerce and mobile gaming companies. Persistent deflation and weak consumption in China have led to low valuations for these stocks, despite robust EBITDA growth and share buyback programs. The Chinese government is shifting towards aggressive fiscal and monetary policies to combat deflation, which should stabilize consumption and re-rate these stocks.

DHS: Avoid in Favor of Other High Yield Dividend ETFs

ValueVole | Sell

The WisdomTree U.S. High Dividend Fund (DHS) offers high dividend yields but suffers from high expense ratios, erratic dividend growth, and poor performance during economic distress. DHS is heavily concentrated in Financials, Energy, and Real Estate sectors, leading to potential underperformance in future economic downturns. Alternatives like Vanguard High Dividend Yield Index Fund (VYM) and Schwab U.S. Dividend Equity ETF (SCHD) offer lower costs, more consistent dividend growth, and better capital preservation.

ITA's Valuation Is Elevated As Its Secular Growth Trend Has Been Priced In

MTS Insights | Hold

The iShares U.S. Aerospace & Defense ETF (ITA) offers exposure to US aerospace and defense companies, with a focus on large-cap stocks and growth investments. The ETF benefits from global military spending trends, particularly NATO's increased defense budgets, despite US fiscal constraints and budgetary pressures. Valuations for ITA are stretched due to recent gains, making it less attractive compared to peers. Given the current valuation and budgetary constraints, I rate ITA as a Hold, preferring (XAR) or (PPA) for new positions in the sector.

The iShares Global Clean Energy ETF: Right Theme, Wrong Instrument

Gaudi Schneider | Sell

Despite its consistent underperformance, the iShares Global Clean Energy ETF (ICLN) is one of the most popular thematic ETF’s with an asset base of 2Bn USD AUM. Investors in the fund are not only exposed to the wind and solar industry, but a range of other risk factors, as well as size and style bias. Even with record public funds financing the transition to clean energy, the fund’s portfolio construction might not make it a targeted enough investment to participate in this theme.

data.title: Prospect Capital: High Yield, But With Caveats
data.description:
Shares of private debt provider Prospect Capital Corporation (NASDAQ:PSEC) are trading at a 40% discount to net asset value (NAV) and provide a current yield of 13.6%. There appears to be a disconnect in pricing given certain metrics, but with 17% of its FY24 interest income derived from payment-in-kind interest, the market doesn’t trust its NAV figure. With investors calling for a share buyback program that would amount to a partial liquidation of the business development corporation, the recent insider buying by the CEO merited a deeper dive. An analysis/recommendation follows below.

Company Overview

Prospect Capital Corporation is a New York City-based business development company [BDC] that provides primarily private debt (and some private equity) to middle-market companies in the U.S. with a current portfolio of 117 investments. It is the largest multi-line BDC, boasting assets of $7.9 billion as of June 30, 2024, having entered into 423 investments since inception. Prospect was both founded as Prospect Street Energy Corporation and taken public in 2004, raising net proceeds of $97.0 million at $15 per share. It changed to its current moniker in 2007 concurrent to terminating its approach of investing at least 80% of its net assets in energy concerns. Shares of PSEC trade at around $5.25 a share, translating to an approximate market cap of $2.2 billion.

The company operates on a fiscal year (FY) ending June 30th. For the avoidance of doubt, the twelve-month period ending June 30, 2024, is FY24.

Business Development Companies

BDCs like Prospect typically elect to be treated as regulated investment companies for federal income tax purposes, allowing them to avoid U.S. federal taxes on all taxable income, provided that they distribute at least 90% of taxable income. Furthermore, BDCs must have gross asset coverage of 150% (or a 2:1 debt-to-equity ratio), which was changed in 2018 from 200% (1:1). This construct allows non-accredited investors access to the private debt market. Prospect’s funding sources are widespread and include publicly traded notes, preferred stock issuance, common stock issuance, and bank financing.

Approach

Prospect technically has no employees, with its assets run by Prospect Capital Management. The screening process for a prospect (no pun intended) to become a credit in the company’s portfolio is rigorous. Its investment professionals evaluate ~3,000 candidates annually, with private equity-sponsored businesses and other intermediaries providing a large source of credit prospects.

That said, the capital management team also employs a dedicated cold-calling effort to leave no stone unturned. The funnel typically consists of concerns with annual revenues less than $750 million and enterprise values less than $1 billion. From that pool of ~3,000, the company structures 200 to 300 deals, of which 20 to 40 close in any given year, meaning less than a 2% book-to-look ratio. Those transactions predominantly include lending to middle-market concerns – many controlled by private equity sponsors – comprising 52% of the portfolio; lending to businesses and purchasing controlling interests in said businesses (19%); purchasing controlling equity positions and lending to real estate investment trusts (19%); and investing in subordinated structured credit (7%).

Portfolio

With this approach, Prospect has a diversified portfolio of 117 investments across 35 industries with 81% first lien, secured, or underlying secured assets. Approximately 89% of investment income comes in the form of interest income, predominantly floating rate and typically with a Secured Overnight Financing Rate [SOFR] plus structure. Real estate, which has come into greater focus in the higher interest rate environment, consists of a wholly owned real estate investment trust [REIT] known as National Property REIT Corp. (“NPRC”). It holds 61 properties, including multifamily, student housing, and senior living. At a fair value of $1.49 billion, it is the largest single component of Prospect’s $7.72 billion investment portfolio.

Market Disconnect

That portfolio generated a 12.1% annualized current yield across performing interest-bearing investments with net debt leverage of 0.45 and a NAV of $8.74 a share, featuring a tiny 0.3% of non-accrual (non-performing) loans – all as of June 30, 2024. With a monthly common dividend of $0.06 (for a current yield of 13.6%) that was seemingly easily covered by FY24 net investment income of $1.02 a share, there would appear to be a significant disconnect between that performance and Prospect’s September 6, 2024, closing share price of $5.28.

However, there are many reasons for the 40% discount to NAV. First, there are many instances in its controlled investment portfolio (and a few in its uncontrolled portfolio) where Prospect isn’t collecting cash interest but rather payment-in-kind (“PIK”). The “interest” collected through PIKs totaled of $134.5 million in FY24, or 17% of the total. Even though this interest is structured, it generates skepticism and uncertainty among investors as to the company’s ‘real’ non-accrual rate.

Next, its portfolio’s net realized and net change in unrealized gains (losses) in FY24 was a loss of $156.8 million after this line item registered a loss of $522.4 million in FY23, largely due to the Chapter 11 filing of credit PGX Holdings (FY23 and FY24 net losses including successor company: $314.0 million). The company also realized losses of $188.6 million in its structured loan portfolio over those two years. Owing to these and other write-offs, NAV has declined from a peak of $10.81 per share in 3QFY22 to $8.74 per share, its lowest level since 1QFY21.

Also included in the losses are $164.4 million (unrealized) from NPRC over the past two years. During the same period, Prospect has raised its debt investment into the REIT by $467.4 million. Although these gross additions can be in the form of loans for new portfolio properties, original issue discount accretion and PIK interest, it has some market participants, including research and investment firm Hedgeye Risk Management, suggesting that the company is filling in the cash flow deficit at NPRC via term loans. In February 2024, Hedgeye stated that the REIT had “not acquired a new property in 7 quarters, over which time PSEC sends over ~$250 million of term loan draws to NPRC”. Speculation that Prospect is propping up its largest investment (19% of its portfolio) has obviously hurt its stock.

Also of note: the coverage of net investment income to common dividend does not factor the preferred dividends into the algebra. Total dividends (preferred and common) paid out in 4QFY24 annualized were $409.0 million or $0.97 per common share, versus net investment income of $1.02 a share in FY24. This is not to suggest anything untoward for management, just that coverage is much tighter upon further inspection, leaving little chance of an increase.

And with the Fed Funds rate likely to be lowered at the next central bank meeting, the interest income in its SOFR-pegged floating-rate portfolio will decline, making the coverage more tenuous. Although not enormous, a 100-basis point drop in interest rates will lower net investment income by $30.3 million or ~$0.07 a share, enough to bring the coverage under 100%. The common dividend, although consistent at $0.06 per month, hasn’t changed since 2017, when it was lowered from $0.08. Factor in general queasiness regarding any commercial real estate loan portfolio and arguments as to why Prospect is trading at a 40% discount to NAV with a just under 14% yield are complete.

4QFY24 Conference Call

More evidence of this somewhat confusing financial reporting occurred when the company posted 4QFY24 net investment income of $0.25 a share, which on the surface looked impressive considering the $0.18 of disbursements to common shareholders in the quarter. However, this figure did not reflect the ~$0.06 a share distributed to preferred shareholders, underscoring the prior point concerning little wriggle room regarding the dividend.

The highlight of the quarter was Prospect executing an amended and extended credit facility from a syndicate of 48 banks, securing commitments totaling $2.1 billion with a 2029 maturity.

On the heels of the PXG Chapter 11 filing, Prospect clarified that it has been rotating out of its second lien assets and subordinated structured notes in favor of additional first lien assets. It was also obvious that co-founder, Chairman, & CEO John Barry was at the end of his patience. He lashed out at Wells Fargo analyst Finian O’Shea in an unhinged rant after the latter posed questions that either reflected Hedgeye’s concerns or (in some instances) were a bit sophomoric for a Wall Street analyst.

Balance Sheet & Analyst Commentary

As stated previously, Prospect’s NAV has fallen to $8.74 a share (as of June 30, 2024), which is down from $8.99 a share on March 30, 2024, and $9.24 a share on June 30, 2023. That said, its publicly traded debt enjoys investment grade ratings from five agencies and the average cost of its total debt is 4.25%.

As for its balance sheet, the company holds debt of $2.46 billion and preferred stock of $1.59 billion against cash of $85.9 million. The gaudy dividend yield of nearly 14% has already been covered. On October 7th, the Prospect Capital announced that it had increased and extended its credit facility to just north of $2 billion.

Part of Barry’s frustration may have to do with the fact that O’Shea, who constitutes the entirety of Street coverage, is not a big fan of Prospect, featuring an underperform rating and a price objective of $4.50. He expects the company’s net investment income to shrink to $0.61 a share in FY25, putting the dividend at risk. O’Shea’s estimate accounts for the preferred dividend, meaning an $0.11 a share shortfall in covering the current $0.06 per month common dividend.

Clearly unconcerned is Barry, who purchased 3.25 million shares at an average price of $5.15 per on August 30 and September 3, 2024. President Eliasek Grier also bought a total of 10,000 shares on those same dates. The CEO now owns over 69 million shares.

Verdict

This is not the first time Barry has made a big purchase in 2024, having acquired 803,000 shares back in May. He is signaling to the Street that he believes in his company. However, with PIKs constituting 17% of total interest income in FY24 and his stock trading at a 40% discount to NAV, shareholders want Prospect to execute a share buyback as proof that its NAV calculation is trustworthy. However, given the nature of BDCs, that request essentially constitutes a partial liquidation, meaning less management and performance fees for Prospect Capital Management, the group charged with investing the company’s assets.

An argument can be made that the Prospect Capital Corporation dividend is safe, and given the discount to NAV, the risk/reward profile is considerably skewed to the upside, making it a solid covered call candidate. However, with the lingering doubt and very little premium in the options, the recommendation is to sit this one out.

data.title: Are Investors Offered A Rare Entry Point Into ASML After Earnings?
data.description:
ASML (OTCQX:ASMIY) identifies itself for investors as the "most important tech company you've never heard of". Indeed, ASML does not share the fame and trendiness of rock star Nvidia, or of other big names in the chips industry such as TSMC, Broadcom, AMD and Intel. But ASML is as important, it is a hidden gem sitting back upstream at a critical part of the value chain of the semiconductor chips industry.

Critical niche and moat-protected business model

ASML was founded in the Netherlands in 1984 - when modern-day technology was in infant stages, and when AI was more reserved for sci-fi futuristic movies. The company has grown to employ 42,000 employees today in more than 60 locations. Last year, the company generated EUR 28 billion in turnover, with a gross margin of 51%, leaving it with a generous EUR 14 billion to invest EUR 4 billion in R&D and in strengthening its niche and expanding its growth. Revenues increased by 130% in the past five years.

ASML has a niche in lithography systems, which use laser light to print patters of microchips. The systems that ASML produces are a mix of hardware and software innovations that are unique to the company. ASML then supplies these systems to semiconductor manufacturers such as Taiwan Semiconductor Manufacturing Company, which produces standardized foundry chips for other companies down the value chain, such as Nvidia, AMD and Qualcomm. ASML has an effective monopoly in producing lithography systems that can manufacture chips smaller than 7nm node, which allows chip manufactures to produce smaller and faster chips.

With 10,000 customers, ASML covers a wide range of industries that use semiconductors, ranging from fast-moving industries related to AI and cloud computing to slower-moving industries such as telecoms, healthcare, automotive and consumer electronics.

Recent results tempered the optimism

This week, the share price of ASML dropped by around 20% in 2 days - so far. The shares now sit where they were at the beginning of the year. The stampede of exit by investors was prompted by an unplanned early announcement of Q3 results that were pretty gloomy - defying all the recent hype about AI's boosting impact on the semiconductor industry. In its announcement, ASML estimated that 2024 full-year sales would be a standstill compared to 2023, with only a minor uplift. 2025 is forecasted by the company to have another minor uplift, with an increase in sales of only 7% on the lower side of the range. The company also reported that gross margins will be subdued - albeit remaining in the 50% range - as the market is subdued and customers are "cautious". New customer orders came in around half expectations, and half the rate of earlier in the year. Adding to that; recent half-year sales dropped by 16%, net income by 18% and operating income margin by 2%.

Even with a 20% drop, the current share price of EUR 650 per share leaves the shares trading at 32x, 2023 P/E. This is a modest valuation for the neck-breaking speed growth that the company was expected to experience. The share price dropped by 37% from the all-time high that was reached only 3 months ago, and is now sitting 18% higher than the 52-week low set exactly 1 year ago in October 2023. The median analyst estimates of share price is EUR 960 per share - providing a 44% upside.

But the recent Q3 results announcement put a harsh brake on expectations of lofty growth. A 30x P/E is definitely not high if growth is in the double-digits range. But if growth reaches a standstill or is in single digits, then there could be concerns that a hefty valuation premium does not have legs. Investors are justified to be cautious; even with the 20% drop, a 32x P/E is still on the high side.

Demand risks

China accounted for around 40% of sales in the past year. Definite slowdown of the Chinese economy and of consumer purchasing power, combined with Western restrictions on China's access to semiconductor technology, are clear contributing factors behind the slowdown of business for ASML.

Adding to that; aside from AI and advanced computing-related sectors, there seems to be a wider slowdown in demand for semiconductor, which could be signs for a wider economic slowdown - not only in China but elsewhere as well.

Fundamentally solid investment, but caution warranted about timing of entry

ASML, without doubt, has a solid business model, that is well-protected by a strong technological and customer-reach moat. The company's financial metrics are very solid; solid revenue generation, superior profitability, and a very strong balance sheet underpinned by an A+ credit rating from Standard and Poor's. ASML is not a high-risk investment. But ASML does not operate in a vacuum; if its industry slows down, so will its growth. Whether the current valuation represents the right entry point, or whether ASML's share price has more to shed, this remains to be seen. For existing investors; there is no need to panic or to consider selling for a loss. For fresh investors, close monitoring is essential to pick the right entry point.
